source_pr.title: privilege/privileges, server: stabalize tests
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?


Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/33245

Problem Summary:

The server test tries to listen on port 4000 / 10080 which are the defaults. So if TiDB is running on the same system it will fail (seems pretty common this might be the case on a developer machine).

The privileges test has leaks. It might be related to the go 1.18 upgrade since nothing has changed.. but it could also be a race that is now common since I had previously seen these errors but they went away.

### What is changed and how it works?

Fixed tests only.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: The privileges test has leaks. It might be related to the go 1.18 upgrade since nothing has changed.. but it could also be a race that is now common since I had previously seen these errors but they went away.
failure_signature: The privileges test has leaks. It might be related to the go 1.18 upgrade since nothing has changed.. but it could also be a race that is now common since I had previously seen these errors but they went away.
root_cause_explanation: The server test was flaky because it bound to default ports (4000/10080), which can conflict with a locally running TiDB or other processes. The privileges test was flaky due to goroutine/leak checks being triggered by background goroutines. This PR stabilizes the tests by using ephemeral ports (`Port=0` / `StatusPort=0`) and by extending goleak ignore rules for known background goroutines.
fix_pattern: Avoid hardcoded ports in tests by binding to port 0 (ephemeral ports); ensure background goroutines are properly cleaned up or explicitly ignored in goleak for known benign goroutines to reduce leak-related flakes.

evidence: evidence: The privileges test has leaks. It might be related to the go 1.18 upgrade since nothing has changed.. but it could also be a race that is now common since I had previously seen these errors but they went away.
